Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
All laboratory values are within normal limits except for a mildly elevated LDL cholesterol (112.8 mg/dL). Glycemic control, renal function, electrolytes, and lipid profile (except LDL) are unremarkable. No evidence of microalbuminuria. Recommend monitoring and considering LDL-lowering strategies if clinically indicated, especially given age and cardiovascular risk.
